title: agropyron repens subsp. junceum
intro: Agropyron repens subsp. junceum, commonly known as couch grass or quackgrass, is a perennial grass species belonging to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include relief from diarrhea, dysentery, and eczema, as well as its use as a diuretic and a treatment for fever. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or taken in capsule form to reap its medicinal benefits.
